name: hasSymbols
description: The published has-symbols package as a vendorable file. Useful when you want the exact symbol support probe locally without keeping the npm dependency.
tagline: Detect real Symbol support across runtimes.
summary: This page ships the published has-symbols file in both raw and normalized forms. The value is to keep a tiny, inspectable symbol support probe local instead of carrying another npm dependency.
nativeAlternative: There is no direct native helper that replaces the package's exact feature probe contract.

variants.label: Normalized
variants.note: Rule-based normalization for modern TS projects. The goal is easier import and build compatibility, not changed behavior.

variants.label: Raw
variants.note: Published package file kept as-is for older teams or repos that still prefer the original CommonJS distribution.

note: Raw and normalized views are both available. Use normalized when you want an ESM and TypeScript-friendly file, and raw when you want the original published package shape.
